what: what does ...args mean in a function
what is the rest syntax in javascript
why: why is rest better than arguments object
how: how to collect extra arguments with rest
how to use rest parameter in function
how to combine named params and rest

1_beginner_explanation.summary: A Rest parameter (`...name`) is a special syntax placed at the **last** parameter position that collects all remaining arguments into a true **Array**. Unlike the legacy `arguments` object, the rest array has full array methods like `forEach`, `map`, `reduce`. It only gathers arguments that do not match earlier named parameters.
1_beginner_explanation.analogy: Imagine hosting a dinner party where you plan for 3 guests (named parameters) but tell the caterer 'and handle any extras as a group' (rest parameter). The first three guests are welcomed by name. Everyone else after that is grouped together under 'additional guests'. The caterer treats that group as a list they can work through systematically.

2_internal_mechanism.execution_flow: During function invocation, the engine assigns the first N arguments to the N named parameters. All remaining arguments — regardless of how many — are collected into a new Array object and bound to the rest parameter name. If no extra arguments are passed, the rest parameter is an empty array `[]`, never `undefined`. The rest parameter is always a genuine Array, giving access to the full Array prototype.
2_internal_mechanism.memory: The rest array is a freshly allocated heap array created per call. It is independent of the caller's data — mutating it inside the function does not affect the caller's arguments list.

3_real_world_example.use_case: Building a flexible logging utility that always requires a log level but accepts any number of message parts.

4_common_confusion.trap: Confusing the rest parameter with the spread operator. Both use `...` but in different positions: rest is in a **function parameter list** (collects into array), spread is in **function calls or array/object literals** (expands an iterable).
4_common_confusion.fix: Rest = collecting many values into one. Spread = expanding one value into many. 'Rest gathers, Spread scatters.'

6_edge_case.quirk: The rest parameter **must** be the last parameter in the list. Placing it anywhere else is a SyntaxError caught at parse time.
